body:
* **Guidelines**
See http://wiki.geneontology.org/index.php/Guidelines_for_GO_textual_definitions#Use_genus-differentia_patterns_for_definitions
More details at https://douroucouli.wordpress.com/2019/07/08/ontotip-write-simple-concise-clear-operational-textual-definitions/


* **GO term ID and label for which you request a definition update**

exocyst
A protein complex peripherally associated with the plasma membrane that determines where vesicles dock and fuse. At least eight complex components are conserved between yeast and mammals. [PMID:27243008, PMID:9700152, PMID:15292201]

* **New proposed definition (genus-differentia pattern, plus additional information if needed)**

exocyst
A ~protein~ VESICLE TETHERING complex peripherally associated with the plasma membrane that determines where vesicles dock and fuse. At least eight complex components are conserved between yeast and mammals. [PMID:27243008, PMID:9700152, PMID:15292201]


* **Reference(s)**
PMID:nnnnnnn

* **Additional information**

remember the term tracker ID

🤖 Working on it...

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/24730520396)

*— @dragon-ai-agent*

Implemented in #31941.

The textual definition of `GO:0000145 ! exocyst` now reads:

> A **vesicle tethering complex** peripherally associated with the plasma membrane that determines where vesicles dock and fuse. At least eight complex components are conserved between yeast and mammals.

The existing axiom `capable_of_part_of GO:0090522 ! vesicle tethering involved in exocytosis` was already in place, so the reasoner correctly derives `is_a GO:0099023 ! vesicle tethering complex` — the new wording now matches the logic. Also added `term_tracker_item` pointing back to this issue.

All SPARQL-QC rules pass and ELK reasoning completes without unsats.
